hops.context: We present here formal arguments that allow us to understand the white-noise model considered in the main text of the paper as a limiting case of certain noise processes of physical relevance.
This is a well-defined limit for an Ohmic reservoir with J(ω) ∝ ω.

question: In the study of noise processes, what is the significance of identifying white noise as a limit of noise processes in the context of an Ohmic reservoir?

options.A: It establishes that white noise can only be derived from quantum mechanical systems, excluding classical interpretations.
options.B: It shows that white noise is unrelated to Ohmic reservoirs and primarily concerns mechanical oscillators.
options.C: It demonstrates that white noise can be modeled as a special case where the spectral density J(ω) is directly proportional to the frequency ω.
options.D: It suggests that white noise is a baseline for all types of noise processes, regardless of their physical properties or spectral densities.

explanation: The correct answer is A because the context implies that in the study of noise processes, particularly for an Ohmic reservoir, white noise is a limiting case where the spectral density J(ω) is proportional to the frequency ω. This relationship forms the basis of understanding how white noise can be modeled within this specific context of noise processes.
